commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: Dost thou wish to see how incapable he is of hurting thee, unless permitted. "These," he saith, "wait all upon Thee, that Thou mayest give them meat in due season" (ver. And this serpent wisheth to devour, but he devoureth not whom he wisheth. Thou hast heard what the serpent's meat is. Thou dost not wish that God give thee to be devoured by the serpent; because not the serpent's food: i. forsake not the Word of God. For where it is said to the serpent, "Dust thou shalt eat," it is said to the transgressor, "Dust thou art, and unto dust thou shall return. " Thou dost not wish to be the serpent's food. be not dust. How, thou repliest, shall I not be dust. If thou hast not a taste for earthly things. Hear the Apostle, that thou mayest not be dust. For the body which thou wearest is earth: but do thou refuse to be earth. What meaneth this. "Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth. " If thou dost not set thy affections on earthly things, thou art not earth: if thou art not earth, thou art not devoured by the serpent, whose appointed food is earth. The Lord giveth the serpent his food when He will, what He will: but He judgeth rightly, he cannot be deceived, He giveth him not gold for earth. "When Thou hast given it them, they gather it.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
